final_answer: Use TLS (wss://) for all WebSocket traffic and authenticate each connection with a short‑lived signed JWT (N1:5). Include a per‑connection nonce (jti) in the JWT and track used nonces to block token replay (N1:6); rotate the token on every reconnect and force re‑authentication (N1:7). For ordering, attach a monotonically increasing sequence number to every message (N1:0) and have the client keep the highest sequence seen, discarding any message with a lower or equal number (N1:1). If messages arrive out of order, buffer them until the missing sequence arrives or request a resend (N1:2). Add a unique message ID (e.g., UUID) to each payload and keep a short‑TTL cache of recent IDs for deduplication (N1:3). Make server‑side handling idempotent by checking the message ID before applying business logic (N1:4). Optionally sign each message with an HMAC using a shared secret to verify integrity (N1:8). Implement exponential backoff for client reconnection attempts to avoid thundering‑herd effects (N1:9).
